expectedDifferences.reason: The local proxy creates a deterministic synthetic MarketWebPresence id while the capture stores Shopify's disposable live id.

notes: HAR-613 coverage for a staged subfolder web presence with defaultLocale en, alternateLocales fr/de, and subfolderSuffix intl. The live capture script enables and restores fr/de locales so Shopify accepts the scenario. Asserts the real live response in cases[5]: Shopify roots each locale at /{language}-{suffix}/ (en-intl, de-intl, fr-intl) and orders rootUrls default-first then alternates sorted alphabetically. (The earlier har613Expected node encoded a /{suffix}/{locale}/ contract that contradicted both the live capture in this same file and the structurally identical single-alternate case in cases[7]; the implementation emulates the real Shopify response rather than that hand-authored contract.)
